title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_53 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 53 the old frozen wrapper is decisively dead, but the preserved moved barrier B = {1,43,47,53} still works: its spill uses only a small factor palette {2,3,5,43,47,53}, leaving exactly 52 complementary primes for C. This points directly to the live movable sparse-barrier / complementary prime-pool theorem shape on the F25 line.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_53 × Z_25) survives bounded rediscovery audit and skeptical local verification, and it materially strengthens the active movable sparse-barrier / complementary prime-pool campaign on the Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line, but it remains an unformalized feeder result rather than an exact Lean-backed theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A movable sparse-barrier / complementary prime-pool slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if one can choose B = {1,q_1,q_2,q_3} with pairwise-coprime barrier primes whose in-interval multiples fit inside the 20 A-slots, and still leave p - 1 primes outside the induced A ∪ B factor palette, then the four-interface bridge theorem yields a prime labeling.
next_action: Run generalize on the active Γ(Z_p × Z_25) campaign using the verified p = 53 feeder, then pressure the same moved-barrier / complementary prime-pool package at z59-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph before any instance-level Lean step.
